quiz_title: Laser Notice No. 50 - IEC Harmonization Quiz Bank

questions.question: What is the document number for Laser Notice No. 50?
questions.explanation: Laser Notice No. 50 is document number 1346.

questions.question: When was Laser Notice No. 50 issued?
questions.explanation: Laser Notice No. 50 was issued on June 24, 2007, superseding the previous version from July 26, 2001.

questions.question: Laser Notice No. 50 is a legally binding regulation.
questions.explanation: Laser Notice No. 50 is a guidance document containing nonbinding recommendations. It does not create or confer any rights for or on any person.

questions.question: Which IEC standard covers medical electrical equipment requirements for lasers?
questions.explanation: IEC 60601-2-22 covers particular requirements for surgical, cosmetic, therapeutic and diagnostic laser equipment. IEC 60825-1 covers general laser product safety.

questions.question: Regardless of IEC conformance, which of the following are ALWAYS required for laser products introduced into U.S. commerce? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All four requirements are mandatory for ALL laser products introduced into U.S. commerce, regardless of IEC conformance claims.

questions.question: How many sections of 21 CFR 1040 can use IEC 60825-1 conformance as an alternative under Laser Notice No. 50?
questions.explanation: Laser Notice No. 50 allows IEC conformance for 17 harmonized sections, including definitions, classification, emission limits, test methods, and various performance requirements.

questions.question: Which of the following sections is UNAFFECTED by Laser Notice No. 50 and still requires FDA compliance?
questions.explanation: Certification (1010.2), Identification (1010.3), and Variances (1010.4) are among the unaffected sections. Classification, safety interlocks, and beam attenuators are harmonized sections.

questions.question: Who is the contact person for Laser Notice No. 50 at FDA/CDRH?
questions.explanation: Jerome Dennis is the contact person for Laser Notice No. 50, reachable at 240-276-3330 or jerome.dennis@fda.hhs.gov.

questions.question: What modified certification statement must manufacturers use on the certification label when claiming IEC conformance under Laser Notice No. 50?
options: Complies with FDA performance standards for laser products except for deviations pursuant to Laser Notice No. 50, dated June 24, 2007
questions.explanation: Manufacturers must use the specific modified statement that mentions deviations pursuant to Laser Notice No. 50 with the date.

questions.question: CDRH has previously issued notices stating non-objection to which of the following? (Select all that apply)
options: Lack of emission indicators on Class II and IIIa systems
Lack of beam attenuators on Class II and IIIa systems
questions.explanation: CDRH has previously stated non-objection to: (1) lack of emission indicators or beam attenuators on Class II and Class IIIa systems, and (2) hazard warning labels as specified in IEC 60825-1.

questions.question: Which editions of IEC 60825-1 are accepted under Laser Notice No. 50?
questions.explanation: Laser Notice No. 50 accepts conformance with IEC 60825-1, Editions 1.2 or 2.

questions.question: The manual reset mechanism requirement (1040.10(f)(10)) is one of the unaffected sections under Laser Notice No. 50.
questions.explanation: Manual reset mechanism (1040.10(f)(10)) is listed as an unaffected section. Laser products must continue to conform to this FDA-specific requirement.

questions.question: Scenario: A manufacturer wants to introduce a laser product that fully complies with IEC 60825-1 Edition 2 for classification, emission limits, and safety interlocks. What additional requirements must they meet for U.S. compliance?
options: Only IEC compliance is needed - no additional FDA requirements
Must add FDA certification label and submit product report, but can use IEC compliance for harmonized sections
Must completely redesign to meet FDA-only requirements
Cannot use IEC compliance for any purpose
questions.explanation: The manufacturer can use IEC compliance for harmonized sections (like classification and interlocks) but must still comply with unaffected FDA requirements including certification, identification, and reporting.

questions.question: In FDA guidance documents, what does the word 'should' indicate?
options: A suggestion or recommendation, but not required
A criminal penalty will result if not followed
questions.explanation: In FDA guidance documents, 'should' means something is suggested or recommended, but not required. Alternative approaches are acceptable if they satisfy applicable statutes and regulations.

questions.question: Demonstration laser products requirements under 1040.11(c) are considered:
options: Only applicable to Class I lasers
questions.explanation: Demonstration laser products (1040.11(c)) are listed as an unaffected section. Manufacturers must conform to these FDA requirements even if they differ from IEC standards.
